# Integration Setup Guide
# Fetched by the standing instructions when Stage 0D is triggered.
# Do not edit this file directly — it is part of the marketing automation system.

STEP 0D — INTEGRATION SETUP

Run this immediately after Stage 0C. This step has TWO PHASES:

PHASE 1 — DISCOVERY: ask the consultant/user a focused set 
of questions across all five integrations upfront, to map 
what already exists in the client's infrastructure. Save 
the answers as a discovery map.

PHASE 2 — SETUP: based on the discovery map, only configure 
the parts of each integration that need configuring. Skip 
work that's already done. Save partially-configured 
integrations as PENDING with handoff notes.

This two-phase approach exists because real client 
infrastructure is usually messy: existing service accounts, 
shared admin accounts, pending API approvals, half-set-up 
tooling. Asking discovery questions first prevents the 
system from telling the user to do work they've already done.

PRE-CHECK — onboarding handoff:
Before starting discovery, check whether the project 
folder contains an integration summary from a pre-Cowork 
onboarding session (typically saved as integration-summary.md 
in /01-client-knowledge/ or attached to the chat by the human).

If a summary exists:
  Use those answers as defaults during discovery. For each 
  question, present the prior answer and ask the human to 
  confirm or update.

If no summary exists: run full discovery from scratch.

NOTE — file path resolution:
Throughout this stage, [client-name] in any file path means 
the actual client folder name in lowercase-with-hyphens format, 
derived from org-knowledge.md. e.g. "Think & Grow" → 
/01-client-knowledge/think-and-grow/integrations.md. Do this 
substitution silently — do not mention [client-name] to the human.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PHASE 1 — DISCOVERY (5 minutes)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Output this intro message, then run through the discovery 
questions one at a time using ask_user_input. The goal is 
a map of existing infrastructure, NOT setup. Do not give 
setup instructions during this phase — just map.

  ---
  INTEGRATION DISCOVERY
  
  Before setting anything up, I need to understand what 
  the client already has in place. This saves time — 
  no point recreating accounts or tokens that already exist.
  
  I'll ask 8-10 questions across analytics, LinkedIn, image 
  generation, Slack, and LinkedIn Ads. After that I'll map 
  out which integrations need full setup, which need partial 
  filling-in, and which are ready as-is.
  
  Should take about 5 minutes. Ready?
  
  Use ask_user_input with:
  "yes \u2014 let's go"
  "skip integration setup for now"
  ---

If "skip integration setup for now": save a minimal 
integrations.md noting that setup was deferred, output:
"All integrations saved as deferred. The weekly workflow 
will run without analytics or auto-publishing. Type 
'Set up integrations' in any future session when ready."
Then skip to STAGE 0D COMPLETE.

If "yes — let's go": proceed to the discovery questions.

DISCOVERY Q1 — Website analytics
Use ask_user_input with:
"GA4 (Google Analytics) installed"
"Cloudflare Analytics installed"
"both installed"
"none installed"
"i don't know"

Save the answer to a working note (do NOT write integrations.md yet).

DISCOVERY Q2 — Analytics ownership (only if Q1 answered with GA4 or Cloudflare)
Use ask_user_input with:
"i have admin access to the analytics account"
"someone at the client has admin access (i'll get credentials from them)"
"shared admin account that requires coordination (e.g. tools@ or admin@)"
"i don't know"

DISCOVERY Q3 — Existing GA4 service account (only if Q1 = GA4)
Output: "Most companies who installed GA4 also created a 
service account for API access at some point. This is 
usually visible in GA4 Admin > Property Access Management. 
Look there now and tell me what you see."
Use ask_user_input with:
"existing service account visible (i can see one)"
"no existing service account"
"i can't access GA4 admin right now"

DISCOVERY Q4 — LinkedIn company page admin
Use ask_user_input with:
"i have admin access to the LinkedIn company page"
"someone else at the client has admin access"
"no LinkedIn company page exists yet"
"i don't know"

DISCOVERY Q5 — LinkedIn developer app
Use ask_user_input with:
"existing developer app with Community Management API approved"
"existing developer app, Community Management API still pending review"
"existing developer app, no Community Management API yet"
"no developer app exists"
"i don't know"

DISCOVERY Q6 — LinkedIn Ads
Use ask_user_input with:
"currently running LinkedIn Ads"
"plan to start LinkedIn Ads in the next 3 months"
"no LinkedIn Ads planned"

DISCOVERY Q7 — Image generation tools
Use ask_user_input with:
"existing branded Canva templates"
"existing Claude Design system"
"both"
"neither \u2014 starting fresh"
"client has an in-house design team \u2014 will brief them externally"

DISCOVERY Q8 — Slack
Use ask_user_input with:
"existing Slack workspace, can dedicate a channel"
"existing Slack workspace, need to create a channel"
"no Slack \u2014 use a different notification method"
"no notifications needed"


DISCOVERY Q9 — Email campaigns and PNG export
Read content-strategy.md now. Check whether the content 
strategy includes email campaigns or newsletters sent via 
an email platform (e.g. HubSpot, Mailchimp, ConvertKit, 
ActiveCampaign or similar).

IF email campaigns or newsletters are present in 
content-strategy.md:
  Set png-needed = yes in working notes.
  Set png-reason = email campaigns require PNG export.
  Do NOT ask the consultant — this is auto-detected.

IF no email content is mentioned in content-strategy.md:
  Use ask_user_input:
  "yes — the client sends email campaigns or newsletters"
  "no — no email campaigns planned"

  If yes: set png-needed = yes, png-reason = consultant confirmed
  If no: set png-needed = no

Note: when png-needed = yes, Canva MCP becomes a mandatory 
Phase 2 prerequisite. Record this in the discovery map.


DISCOVERY Q10 — Email platform
Use ask_user_input with:
"hubspot — we use hubspot for email"
"mailchimp"
"klaviyo"
"convertkit"
"activecampaign"
"another platform — i'll name it"
"no email platform — we don't send campaigns"

If "another platform — i'll name it": ask for the name as 
free text and save it as a custom platform. Flag that 
native integration is not available and metrics will 
require manual entry each month.

If "no email platform — we don't send campaigns": set 
email-platform = none in working notes.

Save the answer to working notes.

DISCOVERY Q11 — Weekly report delivery
The weekly performance report (last week's results + 
competitor activity) runs before idea generation each week.
How would you like to receive it?

Use ask_user_input with:
"slack — post to our channel"
"email — send to my inbox"
"in-chat — show it here each week"

Save the answer to working notes as weekly-report-delivery.

WRITE THE DISCOVERY MAP:
Save the answers to /01-client-knowledge/[client-name]/discovery-map.md
in this format:

```
# Integration Discovery Map — [client name]
Generated: [YYYY-MM-DD]

## Analytics
Tool: [GA4 / Cloudflare / both / none / unknown]
Admin access: [self / client / shared admin / unknown]
Existing GA4 service account: [yes / no / unknown]

## LinkedIn
Company page admin: [self / other / no page / unknown]
Developer app: [approved / pending / no API / none / unknown]
LinkedIn Ads: [running / planned / not planned]

## Image generation
Claude Design system: [exists / doesn't exist]
In-house design team: [yes / no]
png-needed: [yes / no]
png-reason: [email campaigns require PNG export / consultant confirmed / n/a]
email-provider: [HubSpot / Mailchimp / ConvertKit / ActiveCampaign / other / none]
canva-mcp-required: [yes / no]

## Slack
Workspace: [yes / no]
Channel: [exists / needs creating / not needed]

## Email
Platform: [HubSpot / Mailchimp / Klaviyo / ConvertKit / ActiveCampaign / other / none]
Integration status: [to configure / not available / none]

## Reporting preferences
Weekly report delivery: [slack / email / in-chat]
Audit report delivery: [slack / in-chat]
```

Output the discovery map summary in chat and ask the user 
to confirm before moving to Phase 2:

  ---
  DISCOVERY MAP SAVED
  
  [Brief summary of what was discovered]
  
  Based on this, here's what Phase 2 (setup) will do:
  - [GA4: full setup / partial setup / just record credentials / save as pending]
  - [LinkedIn: same structure]
  - [Image generation: same structure]
  - [Slack: same structure]
  - [LinkedIn Ads: same structure]
  
  Ready to proceed?
  
  Use ask_user_input with:
  "yes \u2014 start setup"
  "wait \u2014 i need to update an answer"
  ---

If "wait": ask which answer to update, update the discovery 
map, regenerate the Phase 2 plan, ask to confirm again.

If "yes — start setup": proceed to PHASE 2.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PHASE 2 — SETUP (driven by discovery map)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

For each integration, branch based on the discovery map. 
Skip integrations marked as "not needed" or "not planned" 
entirely — do not surface them at all.

GA4 setup logic:
- If "existing GA4 service account" = yes:
  → Skip account creation. Just ask for the Measurement ID, 
    Property ID, and existing service account email. Save and 
    confirm.
  → Reference INTEGRATION ERROR PATTERNS if any step fails.
- If "existing GA4 service account" = no AND user has admin access:
  → Walk through full service account creation (the original 
    flow from the previous version of these instructions).
  → Reference INTEGRATION ERROR PATTERNS especially for the 
    "service account email rejected" pattern.
- If user can't access GA4 admin right now:
  → Save as PENDING with handoff note: "Service account 
    credentials needed from [admin owner]. Resume by typing 
    'Set up GA4 integration'."
  → Do not block on this — move to the next integration.

(Continue with the rest of the previous integration logic 
but always branch on discovery answers. The full original 
flows still apply when discovery indicates "starting from 
scratch" — they're just gated behind the discovery check.)

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CHECK 1 OF 5 — Google Analytics (GA4)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Output this message exactly:

  ---
  INTEGRATION CHECK — Google Analytics (GA4)

  GA4 connects your website traffic data to the monthly 
  strategy review. When connected, Claude can tell you 
  which blog posts and articles are driving website visits, 
  where traffic is coming from, and whether content is 
  converting to consultation requests.

  Without GA4, the monthly strategy review still works — 
  it just cannot include website performance data.

  Does [client name]'s website use a web analytics tool?

  Use ask_user_input with:
  "yes — google analytics"
  "yes — cloudflare analytics"
  "no analytics set up"
  "skip for now"

  IF "yes — google analytics": follow the GA4 YES flow.
  IF "yes — cloudflare analytics":
    Ask for:
    - Cloudflare Zone ID (found in Cloudflare dashboard → 
      Overview → Zone ID on the right side panel)
    - Cloudflare API token (Cloudflare dashboard → 
      My Profile → API Tokens → Create Token → 
      use "Read analytics" template)
    Save to /01-client-knowledge/[client-name]/integrations.md:
    ## Cloudflare Analytics
    Status: Connected
    Zone ID: [zone_id]
    API token: [token]
    Confirm: "Cloudflare connected. Blog performance 
    will be tracked weekly."
  IF "no analytics set up": follow the GA4 NO flow.
  IF "skip for now": save Status: Skipped.
  ---

Wait for response.

IF YES:
  Ask for the GA4 Measurement ID (format: G-XXXXXXXXXX) 
  and the GA4 API credentials. Save to:
  /01-client-knowledge/[client-name]/integrations.md
  
  Format to save:
  ## GA4
  Status: Connected
  Measurement ID: [G-XXXXXXXXXX]
  Property ID: [numeric ID]
  API credentials: [service account email]
  
  Confirm:
  "GA4 connected. Website performance data will be 
  included in your monthly strategy review automatically."

IF NO:
  Output this message:

  ---
  GA4 SETUP GUIDE

  Setting up GA4 takes about 15 minutes. Here's how:

  Step 1 — Create a GA4 property
  - Go to analytics.google.com
  - Click Admin (bottom left gear icon)
  - Click Create → Property
  - Enter your website name and URL
  - Select your industry and reporting timezone (Australia/Sydney for AU clients)
  - Click Create

  Step 2 — Add the tracking code to your website
  - After creating the property, GA4 gives you a 
    Measurement ID (starts with G-)
  - Copy this ID
  - Add it to your website:
    - Webflow: Site Settings → Integrations → Google Analytics
    - WordPress: Use the Site Kit plugin
    - Custom HTML: Add the gtag.js script to your <head>
    - Shopify: Online Store → Preferences → Google Analytics

  Step 3 — Get API credentials for Cowork
  - Go to console.cloud.google.com
  - Create a new project (or use an existing one)
  - Enable the Google Analytics Data API
  - Go to IAM → Service Accounts → Create Service Account
  - Name it "cowork-analytics" and click Create
  - Click the service account → Keys → Add Key → JSON
  - Download the JSON file
  - Back in GA4: Admin → Property Access Management
  - Add the service account email as a Viewer

  Step 4 — Come back here
  Once setup is complete, type:
  GA4 READY
  and Claude will ask for your Measurement ID and 
  service account credentials to complete the connection.

  Or if you'd prefer to skip GA4 for now, type:
  SKIP GA4
  You can always add it later by typing:
  Set up GA4 integration
  ---

  Wait for GA4 READY or SKIP GA4.
  
  If GA4 READY: follow the YES flow above.
  If SKIP GA4: save to integrations.md:
    ## GA4
    Status: Skipped
    Note: Can be added at any time

IF SKIP:
  Save to /01-client-knowledge/[client-name]/integrations.md:
  ## GA4
  Status: Opted out
  Note: Can be added at any time by typing "Set up GA4 integration"
  
  Confirm: "No problem — the monthly strategy review will 
  run without website performance data."

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CHECK 2 OF 5 — LinkedIn Analytics API
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Output this message exactly:

  ---
  INTEGRATION CHECK — LinkedIn Analytics

  Connecting LinkedIn lets Claude pull your page's follower 
  growth and post performance data automatically each week. 
  This feeds into the monthly strategy review so you can see 
  which content pillars are actually driving engagement.

  Without it, the monthly strategy review uses the 
  marketing log only — it knows what was published 
  but not how it performed.

  To connect LinkedIn you need:
  - A LinkedIn Developer App with Community Management API approved
  - An access token for your LinkedIn Company Page
  - Your LinkedIn Organisation URN

  Do you currently have a LinkedIn developer app set up?

  Use ask_user_input with:
  "yes — app set up and token ready"
  "yes — app set up but no token yet"
  "no — i don't have a developer app"
  "skip for now" 
  ---

Wait for response.

IF YES:
  Ask for:
  - Organisation URN (format: urn:li:organization:XXXXXXX)
  - Access token
  - Token expiry date (tokens expire every 60 days)
  
  Save to /01-client-knowledge/[client-name]/integrations.md:
  ## LinkedIn Analytics
  Status: Connected
  Organisation URN: [urn:li:organization:XXXXXXX]
  Access token: [token]
  Token expiry: [date]
  
  Confirm:
  "LinkedIn connected. Follower stats and post performance 
  will be pulled weekly after Stage 3 and included in your 
  monthly strategy review. Note: your access token expires 
  on [date] — you'll need to refresh it then."

IF NO:
  Output this message:

  ---
  LINKEDIN DEVELOPER APP SETUP GUIDE

  This takes about 20-30 minutes plus a few days waiting 
  for LinkedIn to review your application.

  Step 1 — Create a LinkedIn Developer App
  - Go to linkedin.com/developers/apps
  - Click Create App
  - Enter your app name, link your LinkedIn Company Page, 
    and add a logo
  - Click Create App

  Step 2 — Request Community Management API access
  - In your app, go to the Products tab
  - Find Community Management API
  - Click Access Request Form and complete it
  - Use case: "Marketing analytics — pulling follower 
    and post performance data for internal reporting"
  - LinkedIn will review and email you — usually 3-7 days

  Step 3 — Generate an access token
  Once approved:
  - Go to the Auth tab in your developer app
  - Under OAuth 2.0 tools, click the OAuth token generator
  - Select scopes: r_organization_social, rw_organization_admin
  - Authorise and copy the access token

  Step 4 — Find your Organisation URN
  - Go to your LinkedIn Company Page
  - Click Admin tools → Manage page
  - The URL will contain your org ID: 
    linkedin.com/company/[ID]/admin/
  - Your URN is: urn:li:organization:[ID]

  Step 5 — Come back here
  Once you have your token and URN, type:
  LINKEDIN READY
  and Claude will complete the connection.

  Or to skip for now, type:
  SKIP LINKEDIN
  You can always add it later by typing:
  Set up LinkedIn integration
  ---

  Wait for LINKEDIN READY or SKIP LINKEDIN.
  
  If LINKEDIN READY: follow the YES flow above.
  If SKIP LINKEDIN: save to integrations.md:
    ## LinkedIn Analytics
    Status: Skipped
    Note: Can be added at any time

IF SKIP:
  Save to /01-client-knowledge/[client-name]/integrations.md:
  ## LinkedIn Analytics
  Status: Opted out
  Note: Setup guide available — type "Set up LinkedIn integration"
  
  Confirm: "No problem — the monthly strategy review 
  will run without LinkedIn performance data."

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CHECK 3 OF 5 — Image generation
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Before proceeding, read:
- content-strategy.md — content types planned
- org-knowledge.md — team size, any design team mentioned
- style-guide.md — brand assets, any design tools referenced
- discovery-map.md — png-needed value from Q9

DEFAULT: Claude Design with the client's Design System.
Claude Design is the primary image generation method for 
all clients. Canva MCP is only used as the PNG export 
pipeline when png-needed = yes (email campaigns require 
PNG files for HubSpot/Mailchimp and similar platforms).

CANVA MCP AVAILABILITY CHECK:
Before referencing Canva MCP for PNG export, check 
whether it is available. Use search_mcp_registry with 
query "canva". If a Canva connector is found and 
connected, Canva MCP is available. If not found or 
not connected, note this in working notes:
  canva-mcp-available = yes / no

If png-needed = yes AND canva-mcp-available = no:
  Note that PNG export will require manual export from 
  Claude Design. Flag this to the consultant at the 
  end of Check 3:
  "PNG export for email banners will need to be done 
  manually — export from Claude Design and upload to 
  your email platform directly. Type 'Set up Canva' 
  in the connectors panel to enable automatic export."

RECOMMENDATION LOGIC:

Does the client have a design team or use Adobe / Figma 
/ a professional design stack?
→ Recommend: designer-led. The system generates a 
  design brief each week. Their team produces the image.

Does the client have no existing tools or team?
→ Recommend Claude Design. Note that a one-time 
  Design System setup is required. Takes 10-15 min 
  when brand assets (logo, colours, fonts) are on hand.

CANVA NOTE:
Do not offer Canva templates as an option. Canva MCP 
is used only for PNG export of email banners when 
png-needed = yes and canva-mcp-available = yes. 
This is handled automatically in Stage 2 — it is 
not a separate image generation method.

OUTPUT THIS MESSAGE — include the recommendation 
before the options:

---
All image generation runs through Claude Design using 
the client's Design System — a one-time setup that 
takes 10-15 minutes when you have the brand assets ready.

[OUTPUT RECOMMENDATION HERE — one short paragraph, 
plain language, no jargon. Name the specific content 
types from their strategy and confirm Claude Design 
suits them. If a design team was detected in 
org-knowledge.md, recommend designer-led instead.
Example:

"Your content mix is mostly thought leadership and 
campaign posts — all novel content where Claude Design 
works well. Once the Design System is set up with 
the brand colours and fonts, I'll generate a design 
brief for each post each week."]

Use ask_user_input with:
"set up claude design now (10-15 min — have brand assets ready)"
"skip for now — set up claude design later"
"my design team handles images — generate a brief"
"another tool — i'll tell you which"
---

IF "set up claude design now":

Read discovery-map.md — check whether an existing 
Claude Design system was noted in Q7.

If existing Design System found:
  Output: "A Design System already exists for this client. 
  I just need the name so I can reference it each week."
  Ask for the Design System name as free text.
  Save to /01-client-knowledge/[client-name]/integrations.md:
  ## Image generation
  Method: claude-design
  Design system: [name provided]
  Design system status: existing
  (then proceed to PNG export path below)

If no existing Design System:
  Output this message:

  ---
  Claude Design produces on-brand images when it has 
  a Design System to work from — your brand colours, 
  fonts, and visual style. Without one, it generates 
  something generic.

  Setting up a Design System takes 10-15 minutes when 
  you have the brand assets ready. I'll walk you 
  through it now.

  Step 1 — Go to claude.ai/design in your browser.
  The client needs a paid Claude account for this step.
  Sign in to the client's Claude account (not yours).
  ---

  Use ask_user_input: "done — i'm in claude design" / "i'm stuck" / "the client doesn't have a paid claude account"

  If "the client doesn't have a paid claude account":
    Output: "The client needs a paid Claude account 
    to use Claude Design. Once they have one, come back 
    to this step. For now I'll save image generation 
    as pending."
    Save to integrations.md:
    ## Image generation
    Method: claude-design
    Design system status: pending — client needs paid Claude account
    Export path: pending
    Skip to CHECK 4.

  [Walk through Design System setup step by step:
  1. Open Design > Settings > Create Design System
  2. Name it after the client (e.g. "Think and Grow")
  3. Upload brand logo
  4. Set primary and secondary brand colours
     (hex codes — read from style-guide.md if available)
  5. Set font choices (read from style-guide.md if available)
  6. Set visual style: photography / illustration / 
     abstract / geometric / minimalist
     (read from style-guide.md if available)
  7. Save
  Confirm each step before proceeding. One step at a time.]

PNG EXPORT PATH (applies after Design System is configured):

Read discovery-map.md — check png-needed.
Read working notes — check canva-mcp-available.

If png-needed = yes AND canva-mcp-available = yes:
  Output: "Because this client sends email campaigns, 
  images need to be exported as PNG files for the email 
  platform. Canva MCP is connected — I'll handle the 
  PNG export automatically each week. No action needed."
  Save to integrations.md (add to Image generation section):
  Export path: send-to-canva
  PNG export: required
  PNG reason: [png-reason from discovery-map.md]
  Canva MCP: available

If png-needed = yes AND canva-mcp-available = no:
  Output: "This client sends email campaigns, so images 
  need to be exported as PNG files. Canva MCP isn't 
  connected — you'll need to export manually from Claude 
  Design for now. To enable automatic export, add Canva 
  from the connectors panel."
  Save to integrations.md (add to Image generation section):
  Export path: manual
  PNG export: required
  PNG reason: [png-reason from discovery-map.md]
  Canva MCP: not available — manual export until connected

If png-needed = no:
  Output: "I'll generate a design brief each week that 
  you paste into Claude Design. Once exported, drop the 
  image into your drafts folder."
  Save to integrations.md (add to Image generation section):
  Export path: self
  PNG export: not required
  Canva MCP: not required

Once all above is saved:
Save complete integrations.md Image generation block:
## Image generation
Method: claude-design
Design system: [name]
Design system status: configured
Export path: [send-to-canva / manual / self]
PNG export: [required / not required]
Canva MCP: [available / not available / not required]
Confirm: "Claude Design is set up. I'll generate a 
design brief for each post each week. [If png required: 
Email banner images will [export via Canva automatically 
/ need to be exported manually from Claude Design].]"

IF "skip for now — set up claude design later":
Save to /01-client-knowledge/[client-name]/integrations.md:
## Image generation
Method: claude-design
Design system status: pending — setup deferred
Export path: pending
Confirm: "No problem. Image generation saved as pending. 
Type 'Set up image generation' in any future session 
when ready. Weekly drafts will include design briefs 
but images won't be confirmed until setup is complete."

IF "my design team handles images — generate a brief":
Ask one question:

---
What tool does your design team work in?

Use ask_user_input with:
"adobe (illustrator / photoshop / express)"
"figma"
"canva"
"another tool"
---

Save the answer, then:
Save to /01-client-knowledge/[client-name]/integrations.md:
## Image generation
Method: designer-brief
Design tool: [adobe / figma / canva / other]
Export path: manual
Note: design team produces images from weekly brief
Confirm: "Got it. Each week I'll generate a 
detailed image brief your design team can take 
straight into [tool]. Once the image is ready, 
drop it in your drafts folder and type: Images ready"

IF "another tool — i'll tell you which":
Ask the human to name the tool as free text.
Do a web search: "[tool name] MCP connector Claude 2026"
If an MCP connector exists:
  Present it via suggest_connectors.
  Wait for the human to connect it.
  Once connected, save as Method: [tool name], 
  Export path: mcp.
If no MCP connector:
  Do a web search: "[tool name] API image export 2026"
  Based on results:
  - Explain whether it has an API that could be integrated
  - Note clearly that it is not currently built in and 
    images will need to be exported manually
  Save to integrations.md:
  ## Image generation
  Method: [tool name]
  Export path: manual
  Note: not natively integrated — manual export required
  Confirm: "Each week I'll generate a design brief 
  you can take into [tool]. Drop the exported image 
  in your drafts folder and type: Images ready"

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CHECK 4 OF 5 — Slack notifications
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Output this message:

  ---
  Slack notifications keep you in the loop without you 
  needing to open Cowork. When connected, I'll send a 
  short message when this week's ideas are ready to 
  review, when drafts are ready, and when the monthly 
  performance report is published.
  
  Without it, the workflow still runs — you'll just need 
  to check Cowork directly to see when each stage finishes.
  
  Do you want Slack notifications?
  
  Use ask_user_input with:
  "yes — i have a channel to use"
  "yes — but i need to create a channel first"
  "skip for now"
  ---

Wait for response.

IF "yes — i have a channel to use":
  Ask:
  ---
  Which Slack channel should notifications go to?
  Type the channel name including the # 
  e.g. #marketing or #content-team
  ---
  
  Wait for the channel name.
  
  Save to /01-client-knowledge/[client-name]/integrations.md:
  ## Slack notifications
  Status: Connected
  Channel: [channel name]
  
  Confirm: "Slack connected. I'll post to [channel] when 
  ideas, drafts, and reports are ready."

IF "yes — but i need to create a channel first":
  Output:
  ---
  CREATE A SLACK CHANNEL
  
  Step 1 — Open Slack
  Click the + next to Channels in the left sidebar.
  
  Step 2 — Create the channel
  Name it something memorable e.g. #marketing-automation 
  or #[company]-content. Mark it private if you want 
  only specific people to see notifications.
  
  Step 3 — Make sure Claude has access
  If the channel is private, add the Claude integration 
  by typing /add @claude in the channel.
  
  Step 4 — Come back here
  Type the channel name including the # 
  e.g. #marketing-automation
  ---
  
  Wait for the channel name, then save the same way as above.

IF "skip for now":
  Save to /01-client-knowledge/[client-name]/integrations.md:
  ## Slack notifications
  Status: Skipped
  Note: Can be added later by typing "Set up Slack integration"
  
  Confirm: "No Slack notifications for now. You'll get 
  workflow updates inside Cowork directly."

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CHECK 5 OF 5 — LinkedIn Ads and boost preference
─────────────────────────────────────────────────

Output this message:

---
Do you run or plan to run LinkedIn Ads?

Use ask_user_input with:
"yes — ads are already running"
"yes — we plan to start ads soon"
"no ads planned"
---

IF "yes — ads are already running" OR "yes — we plan to start ads soon":
  Ask:
  ---
  When a post performs well organically, I can flag it
  as a candidate to promote as a paid ad.
  How would you like that to work?

  Use ask_user_input with:
  "ai decides — flag top performers automatically"
  "i decide — show me all posts and i will choose"
  ---

  IF "ai decides — flag top performers automatically":
    Ask: "I'll flag posts in the top 20% by engagement
    rate as boost candidates after 14 days live.
    Does that threshold work?

    Use ask_user_input with:
    'yes — use top 20 percent'
    'no — let me set a different threshold'
    ---
    IF 'no': ask for their threshold. Use their number.

    Save to integrations.md:
    ## LinkedIn Ads
    Status: Active
    Boost mode: auto-flag
    Boost threshold: top [%] by engagement rate
    Review window: 14 days after publish
    Confirm: "I'll automatically flag your top performers
    as boost candidates after 14 days."

  IF "i decide — show me all posts and i will choose":
    Save to integrations.md:
    ## LinkedIn Ads
    Status: Active
    Boost mode: human-review
    Boost threshold: none
    Review window: 14 days after publish
    Confirm: "Each month I'll show you all posts and
    you can choose which to boost."

  Then ask:
  ---
  Is your LinkedIn Ads account already set up?

  Use ask_user_input with:
  "yes — ads account exists"
  "no — not set up yet"
  ---

  IF "yes — ads account exists":
    Walk through setup step by step:

    Step 1 — Open LinkedIn Campaign Manager
    Go to: linkedin.com/campaignmanager

    Step 2 — Find your Sponsored Account URN
    Go to Account Assets > Account Settings.
    Your Account ID is in the URL bar.
    Format as: urn:li:sponsoredAccount:[your-id]

    Step 3 — Generate a token with Ads scopes
    Go to linkedin.com/developers/apps > your app > Auth.
    Add these scopes: rw_ads, r_ads_reporting,
    r_organization_social
    Generate a new token.

    Step 4 — Type:
    Set up LinkedIn Ads integration [account-urn] [token]

    Wait for trigger. Then save to integrations.md:
    ## LinkedIn Ads
    Sponsored Account URN: [urn]
    Ads Access Token: [token]
    Ads Token Expiry: [today + 60 days]

  IF "no — not set up yet":
    Output: "When you're ready, type:
    Set up LinkedIn Ads integration
    I'll walk you through it then."
    Save to integrations.md:
    ## LinkedIn Ads
    Status: Planned — not yet configured

IF "no ads planned":
  Save to integrations.md:
  ## LinkedIn Ads
  Status: Not applicable
  Confirm: "No ads. Ad creation and boost features
  won't appear in your workflow."

─────────────────────────────────────────────────
ONCE ALL CHECKS ARE COMPLETE:
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Output:

  ---

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CHECK 6 OF 6 — Email platform
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Read discovery-map.md — check ## Email -> Platform.

If Platform = none: skip this check silently.

If Platform is set: proceed.

MCP CONNECTOR CHECK (run before any manual API setup):
Use search_mcp_registry with the platform name as the 
query (e.g. "HubSpot", "Mailchimp").

If an official MCP connector is found:
  Present it via suggest_connectors.
  Output this alongside the connector prompt:

  ---
  EMAIL PLATFORM — [Platform name]

  Connecting your email platform lets the system track 
  newsletter performance automatically — open rates, 
  click rates, and list growth feed into the monthly 
  performance report.

  [Platform] has an official connector. Click Connect 
  to link it — it will ask you to sign in and authorise 
  read access. No tokens or API keys needed.

  When choosing permissions, select read-only scopes:
  - View email campaign data (opens, clicks, unsubscribes)
  - View contact list data (list size, growth)
  - View basic revenue/marketing metrics
  Do NOT grant write permissions — this system only 
  reads data, never creates or modifies records.
  ---

  Wait for the human to connect.

  Once connected, make a test call using the MCP 
  connector to verify access (fetch recent campaigns 
  or list summary — any read call that confirms auth).

  If test call succeeds:
    Save to /01-client-knowledge/[client-name]/integrations.md:
    ## Email platform
    Platform: [platform]
    Connection method: MCP connector
    Status: connected
    Confirm: "[Platform] connected. Newsletter performance 
    data will appear in the monthly report."

  If test call fails:
    Reference INTEGRATION ERROR PATTERNS.
    Save as PENDING with error noted.

If no MCP connector found:
  Fall back to manual API key setup:

  Output:
  ---
  EMAIL PLATFORM — [Platform name]

  [Platform] doesn't have a native MCP connector, so 
  we'll connect it using an API key instead.

  [HUBSPOT FALLBACK:]
  In HubSpot: Settings → Integrations → Private Apps 
  → Create a private app. Name it "Marketing Automation 
  — [Client Name]". Under Scopes, add read access for:
    crm.objects.contacts.read
    marketing-email
    content
  Click Create app, copy the token (starts with 
  pat-na1- or pat-eu1-). Paste it below.

  [MAILCHIMP FALLBACK:]
  In Mailchimp: Profile icon → Account → Extras → 
  API keys → Create A Key. Name it "Marketing Automation 
  — [Client Name]". Copy the key. Also note your 
  data center prefix (e.g. us14 — visible in your 
  Mailchimp URL). Paste key below.

  [OTHER PLATFORM:]
  Check whether [platform] has an API for campaign 
  reporting. If yes, ask the human to locate the API 
  key in their account settings and paste it below.
  If no API available, save as manual entry.
  ---

  Ask the human to paste the key as free text.
  Save key to integrations.md under ## Email platform.
  Test the connection with one read API call.
  If successful: Status = connected (API key).
  If failed: reference INTEGRATION ERROR PATTERNS, 
  save as PENDING.

IF HUMAN SKIPS or platform has no API:
Save to /01-client-knowledge/[client-name]/integrations.md:
## Email platform
Platform: [platform name]
Connection method: manual entry
Status: manual entry
Note: paste campaign stats monthly when prompted
Confirm: "Saved as manual entry. Each month I'll 
prompt you to paste your campaign stats."

  STAGE 0D COMPLETE — INTEGRATIONS CONFIGURED

  Integration status:
  GA4: [Connected / Skipped / Opted out]
  LinkedIn: [Connected / Skipped / Opted out]
  LinkedIn Ads: [Active — auto-flag / Active — human-review / Planned / Not applicable]
  Image generation: [Canva / Claude Design / other / none]
  Slack: [Connected — #channel / Skipped]

  Your integration settings are saved to:
  /01-client-knowledge/[client-name]/integrations.md

  You can add or update any integration at any time 
  by typing "Set up [GA4 / LinkedIn / Slack] integration"

  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  STAGE 0 IS NOW FULLY COMPLETE
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Everything is set up. The weekly content workflow 
  is ready to run.

  TO START YOUR FIRST WEEK OF CONTENT:
  Type exactly this:

  Run Stage 1

  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  QUICK REFERENCE — what to type at each stage:
  
  Start idea generation:        Run Stage 1
  Start draft production:       Run Stage 2
  Start publishing prep:        Run Stage 3
  Run monthly review:           Run monthly strategy review
  Add GA4 later:                Set up GA4 integration
  Add LinkedIn later:           Set up LinkedIn integration
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ---

STAGE 0 IS NOW COMPLETE. Do not run Stage 0 again for this 
client unless the strategy needs a full reset.

---

RUN PERFORMANCE BRIEF

Trigger: Type "Run performance brief"
Best run: Sunday evening or early Monday before Stage 1.
Purpose: Fetch all performance data, identify patterns,
and write a brief that Stage 1 and Stage 2 use to weight
ideas and copy toward what is actually working.

PREREQUISITE CHECK:
Read integrations.md. If nothing is connected (no LinkedIn,
no GA4, no content log), output:
"No analytics connected yet. The performance brief needs
at least LinkedIn or GA4.
Type: Set up LinkedIn integration or Set up GA4 integration"
Then stop.

STEP 1 — COLLECT RAW DATA

Content log (always available):
Read /06-published/marketing-log.csv — last 28 days.
Group posts by pillar, format (infer: carousel / single-image /
text-only from image filename), topic.

Ideas history (always available):
Read all CSVs in /03-ideas/.
Calculate approval rate by pillar over last 60 days.

LinkedIn organic (if connected):
Fetch post analytics for last 28 days.
For each post extract: impressions, likes, comments,
shares, clicks, engagement rate.
Cross-reference URNs with marketing-log.csv for pillar and topic.
Classify first line of copy as hook type:
question / bold-statement / statistic / story / list / other

GA4 (if connected):
Fetch last 28 days of top 5 pages by sessions.
Note which topics those pages cover.

LinkedIn Ads (if active):
Read /06-published/linkedin-ads.csv last 28 days.
Note top and bottom performers by CTR and engagement rate.
Note which angles and copy styles drove conversions.

Email platform (if connected):
Read integrations.md — check ## Email platform -> Status.
If Status = connected and Platform = HubSpot:
  Call: GET https://api.hubapi.com/marketing/v3/emails?limit=10&orderBy=-sendDate
  Header: Authorization: Bearer [token from integrations.md]
  For each campaign sent in last 28 days extract:
    campaign name, send date, recipients, opens (unique),
    open rate, clicks (unique), click rate, unsubscribes
  Also call: GET https://api.hubapi.com/crm/v3/objects/contacts?limit=1
  Read x-total-count header for total list size.
  Calculate list growth: compare to prior brief if available.

If Status = connected and Platform = Mailchimp:
  Call: GET https://[dc].api.mailchimp.com/3.0/campaigns?count=10&status=sent&sort_field=send_time&sort_dir=DESC
  Header: Authorization: apikey [key from integrations.md]
  For each campaign extract: id, send_time, emails_sent.
  Then for each campaign id:
  GET https://[dc].api.mailchimp.com/3.0/reports/[campaign_id]
  Extract: opens.open_rate, clicks.click_rate, 
  unsubscribes.count, bounces.hard_bounces.
  Also call: GET https://[dc].api.mailchimp.com/3.0/lists
  Sum total_subscribers across lists for total list size.

If Status = manual entry or platform not yet supported:
  Check /06-published/performance-brief-*.md for most 
  recent manually entered email stats.
  If found: carry forward with note "[MANUALLY ENTERED]".
  If not found: omit email section from brief.

If Status not set or ## Email platform missing from 
integrations.md: skip silently.

STEP 2 — PATTERN ANALYSIS

PILLAR PERFORMANCE:
Calculate average engagement rate per pillar.
Rank highest to lowest.
Flag any pillar with under 50% idea approval rate
(may be ideas off-brief, not pillar weakness).

FORMAT PERFORMANCE:
Average engagement rate per format.
Identify: best performing / most overused / underused opportunity.

HOOK TYPE PERFORMANCE:
Average engagement rate per hook type.
Identify which opening style works best for this audience.

TOPIC RESONANCE:
Which topics drove above-average engagement.
What are the underlying themes?

CONTENT GAPS:
GA4 topics with traffic not covered on LinkedIn recently.
Ad angles that worked but haven't been tested organically.
Pillars not posted in last 14 days.

STEP 2.5 — READ LEARNINGS FILE

Before writing the brief, read:
/01-client-knowledge/[client-name]/learnings.md

If the file exists, extract the most recent entry's:
- Recommended adjustments for next month
- Format signals (what outperformed)
- Hook signals (what drove engagement)
- Pillar signals (what's gaining or losing traction)

Hold these in working notes as LEARNINGS SIGNALS.
They surface in the brief output in Step 3 below.
Do not output them now.

If the file does not exist: proceed without it.

STEP 3 — WRITE THE BRIEF

Save to /06-published/performance-brief-[YYYY-MM-DD].md:

---
PERFORMANCE BRIEF — [date]
Data window: last 28 days
Sources: [list connected sources]

PILLAR RANKINGS (by engagement rate):
[Rank all pillars with avg engagement % and UP/DOWN/STABLE]

Recommendation: Weight ideas toward [top pillars] this week.
[Lowest pillar] — consider refreshing angle or format.

FORMAT SIGNALS:
Best: [format] — avg [X]% engagement
Overused: [format] — engagement declining
Underused opportunity: [format] — strong when used
Recommendation: [specific format guidance]

HOOK TYPE SIGNALS:
Top hook for this audience: [type] — avg [X]%
Weakest hook: [type] — consider retiring
Recommendation: Lead with [hook type] for priority posts

  [If learnings.md was found and has a current entry:]
  ────────────────────────────────────────────
  LEARNINGS FROM LAST MONTH
  [List the recommended adjustments from learnings.md 
  as bullet points — exactly as written in the file.
  No interpretation or expansion.]
  These signals have been applied to this week's 
  idea generation.
  ────────────────────────────────────────────

TOPIC RESONANCE:
[Top 2-3 themes with which posts and engagement]
Adjacent angles to explore: [2-3 specific suggestions]

CONTENT GAPS:
[List each gap — GA4 topic, ad angle, dormant pillar]

AD PERFORMANCE NOTES (if ads active):
Top converting angle: [description]
Organic-to-ad candidates: [list posts meeting threshold,
or note human-review mode]

OVERALL SIGNAL FOR THIS WEEK:
[2-3 plain sentences. What to do more of, less of,
and try for the first time. Written for a consultant.]
---

STEP 4 — CONFIRM TO HUMAN

---
Performance brief ready for [date].

Top pillar: [pillar] ([X]% avg engagement)
Best hook: [hook type]
Key gap: [one gap in one line]

Type: Run Stage 1 to continue.
---

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
